summary: Verified iteration 76 goal (Spanish URLs added to /app/frontend/src/data/relatedRoutes.js so 'Otros traslados populares' on Spanish city transfer pages is 100% Spanish). Results: PARTIAL PASS. Pages that consume the shared registry via CityTransferPage (ES CDG, ES Orly) now correctly render 7 all-Spanish /es/... links with no English fallback (previously 5 ES + 2 EN in iteration 75). Regressions on RU CDG and EN CDG still PASS (all same-language links). HOWEVER, the ES Nice (/es/traslado-aeropuerto-niza) and ES Gare de Lyon (/es/traslado-estacion-gare-de-lyon-paris) pages requested in tests 2, 3, and 5 do NOT consume the relatedRoutes.js registry at all — they are custom pages rendered by EsServicePage (via EsNice.js / EsGareDeLyon.js) that ship their own hardcoded `relatedLinks` prop (4 static links inside 'Tambien te puede interesar' block, NOT 'Otros traslados populares'). Adding es-URLs for these routes in relatedRoutes.js has no effect on those pages.

integration_issues.flow: Spanish city transfer pages related-routes block
integration_issues.issue: The following /es/... routes are wired in App.js to custom EsServicePage-based components (EsNice, EsCannes, EsMonaco, EsBarcelona, EsAlicante, EsRome, EsMilan, EsBerlin, EsMunich, EsGareDuNord, EsGareDeLyon, EsGareSaintLazare, EsGareMontparnasse, EsGareAusterlitz). These pages do NOT import CityTransferPage or getRelatedRoutes; they render a locally-hardcoded 'Tambien te puede interesar' section with 4 links from a `relatedLinks` prop. So the newly-added Spanish URLs in relatedRoutes.js for gare-de-lyon/gare-du-nord/gare-montparnasse/gare-saint-lazare/gare-est/nice/cannes/monaco/milan/rome/barcelona/alicante/berlin/munich never render on these bespoke Spanish pages. The iteration 76 data change only benefits registry-driven pages (ES CDG, ES Orly, ES Beauvais, ES Disneyland, ES Paris Airport, ES Paris Train Stations, ES Yerevan) where it works perfectly.

test_results.test_1_es_cdg_related_block: PASS - /es/traslado-aeropuerto-charles-de-gaulle now shows 7 links, ALL with /es/... hrefs and hreflang=es. Exact URLs match spec: /es/traslado-aeropuerto-orly, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-beauvais, /es/traslado-disneyland-paris, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-paris, /es/traslado-estaciones-tren-paris, /es/traslado-estacion-gare-de-lyon-paris, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-niza. Labels all Spanish (Paris Orly, Disneyland Paris, Aeropuertos de Paris, Estaciones de tren de Paris, Estacion Gare de Lyon, Aeropuerto de Niza). Section title 'Otros traslados populares'. Regression from iteration 75 (which had 2 EN fallbacks) is fixed.
test_results.test_2_es_nice_related_block: FAIL - /es/traslado-aeropuerto-niza does NOT render the registry-driven Related block. Page uses EsServicePage component (EsNice.js) with 4 hardcoded relatedLinks: Traslado aeropuerto Cannes, Traslado aeropuerto Monaco, Conductor privado Paris, Minivan privado. Section title is 'Tambien te puede interesar' not 'Otros traslados populares'. No [data-testid^=related-route-] elements exist on this page. Adding Spanish URL for 'nice' to relatedRoutes.js has zero effect here.
test_results.test_3_es_gare_de_lyon_related_block: FAIL - /es/traslado-estacion-gare-de-lyon-paris does NOT render the registry-driven Related block. Page uses EsServicePage (EsGareDeLyon.js) with 4 hardcoded relatedLinks visible in screenshot: Traslado Gare du Nord, Traslado aeropuerto Orly, Traslado Disneyland Paris, Minivan privado. Section title 'Tambien te puede interesar'. No [data-testid^=related-route-] elements. Adding Spanish URL for 'gare-de-lyon' to relatedRoutes.js has zero effect on this page.
test_results.test_4_regression_ru_en_cdg: PASS - RU /taksi-iz-aeroporta-cdg still shows 7 Russian links (all hreflang=ru, Cyrillic labels, RU URLs). EN /cdg-airport-transfer still shows 7 English links (hreflang=en). No ES leakage. Registry additive change did not regress existing languages.
test_results.test_5_no_404_navigation: PARTIAL PASS - ES CDG → click 'related-route-orly' navigates to /es/traslado-aeropuerto-orly which loads correctly (H1 'Traslado aeropuerto Orly a Paris | Precio fijo | ZONT') and itself renders 7 /es/... related-route links (registry-driven via OrlyTransfer). The ES Nice→ES Cannes and ES Gare de Lyon→ES Gare du Nord navigation clicks were not testable in the same way because those pages don't have the [data-testid^=related-route-*] structure — they use different hardcoded links (which do navigate correctly to the same targets, but not through the registry system this iteration was meant to validate).

action_items: PRIMARY DECISION FOR MAIN AGENT: The Spanish routes /es/traslado-aeropuerto-niza, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-cannes, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-monaco, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-milan, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-roma, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-barcelona, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-alicante, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-berlin, /es/traslado-aeropuerto-munich, /es/traslado-estacion-gare-du-nord-paris, /es/traslado-estacion-gare-de-lyon-paris, /es/traslado-estacion-gare-saint-lazare-paris, /es/traslado-estacion-gare-montparnasse-paris, /es/traslado-estacion-gare-austerlitz-paris are wired in App.js to custom EsServicePage-based components (not CityTransferPage). To make the iteration 76 goal effective on these pages, either: (a) change App.js routes to point to the shared registry-driven CityTransferPage-based components (like was already done for CDG/Orly/Beauvais/Disneyland/ParisAirport/ParisTrainStation/Yerevan), OR (b) refactor EsServicePage to also consume getRelatedRoutes() from relatedRoutes.js instead of the hardcoded `relatedLinks` prop, OR (c) manually update the hardcoded `relatedLinks` arrays in each Es*.js file to match the new registry entries (and use `Otros traslados populares` as the section title).
If the iteration goal was scoped only to the shared-registry pages (ES CDG in particular), then iteration 76 achieved its goal for that page and this is more of a follow-up gap than a blocker.
critical_code_review_comments: Duplication: The Spanish city transfer surface has two parallel implementations (shared CityTransferPage vs. custom EsServicePage). This has already caused this iteration's mismatch. Consider consolidating: convert all Es*.js pages to use CityTransferPage with pageId prop, and delete EsServicePage. This would make relatedRoutes.js the single source of truth for cross-linking, guarantee same-language 100% coverage, and simplify future SEO changes.
The registry entry 'gare-est' still maps to the /es/traslado-estacion-gare-austerlitz-paris URL (id/URL mismatch noted in iteration 75). Rename id to 'gare-austerlitz' for clarity. Non-blocking.

Registry additions in this iteration are syntactically correct — labels are localized (Estación Gare de Lyon, Aeropuerto de Niza, Aeropuerto de Múnich, etc.), URLs correctly point to existing routes in App.js. No typos observed.

success_rate.frontend: ~55% (2/5 review-request tests PASS, 1 PARTIAL, 2 FAIL — but the failures are because the target pages don't consume the registry the iteration modified)

context_for_next_testing_agent: relatedRoutes.js registry updates only affect pages rendered through the shared CityTransferPage (or its city-specific wrappers like CDGTransfer, OrlyTransfer). Bespoke Es*.js pages using EsServicePage have their own hardcoded relatedLinks prop and DO NOT consult the registry. To spot-check registry effectiveness on any /es/... page: (1) confirm the App.js route points to a CityTransferPage-based component, (2) then check `section[aria-label='Related transfer routes'] [data-testid^=related-route-]`. If step (1) points to an Es*.js component, the registry change won't apply — check the .js file's `relatedLinks` prop instead.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration 76 added Spanish URL entries to /app/frontend/src/data/relatedRoutes.js for gare-de-lyon/gare-du-nord/gare-montparnasse/gare-saint-lazare/gare-est/nice/cannes/monaco/milan/rome/barcelona/alicante/berlin/munich. This achieves 100% Spanish coverage for pages rendered via the shared CityTransferPage component. But the Spanish routes for those specific cities in App.js are wired to bespoke Es*.js components using EsServicePage, which never call getRelatedRoutes(). Hence ES Nice / ES Gare de Lyon still show their old hardcoded 4-link 'Tambien te puede interesar' block. Fix: either re-wire the App.js routes to the shared components, or make EsServicePage consume the registry, or update the hardcoded relatedLinks props.
